result.summary: Notion exposes a developer REST API for workspace content and an official CLI; authentication options documented in the corpus include internal connection API tokens, personal access tokens, public OAuth 2.0 connections, and CLI login sessions.

notion_internal_connection_token.label: Notion internal connection API token
notion_internal_connection_token.setup: Create an internal connection in the [Notion developer portal](https://developers.notion.com/) and copy its static API token. Internal connections are scoped to a single workspace and use a static bearer token. Send it as `Authorization: Bearer …`.

notion_pat.label: Notion personal access token
notion_pat.setup: Create a personal access token from the [Notion developer portal](https://developers.notion.com/). Personal access tokens are user-scoped tokens for scripts, CLI workflows, Workers, and trusted tools, and use the creator’s workspace membership and page permissions. Send it as `Authorization: Bearer …`.

notion_public_oauth.label: Notion public connection OAuth 2.0 credential
notion_public_oauth.setup: Create a public connection in the [Notion developer portal](https://developers.notion.com/). Public connections use OAuth 2.0 for authentication and can be installed in any workspace or selected workspaces only. Users grant access during the OAuth flow, including page selection.

notion_cli_session.label: Notion CLI login session
notion_cli_session.setup: Use the official Notion CLI referenced in the [developer portal](https://developers.notion.com/) under `CLI Reference`. Acquire auth by logging in with the CLI; the corpus confirms CLI workflows are a supported use case for personal access tokens and documents a CLI surface, but does not provide the exact login command.

surfaces.name: Notion REST API
surfaces.notes: The corpus states Notion API is a REST API and that internal connections use a static API token, public connections use OAuth 2.0, and personal access tokens are static bearer tokens. No machine-readable OpenAPI URL or base API URL appears in the corpus.

surfaces.name: Notion CLI
surfaces.notes: The developer portal includes a `CLI Reference` section and explicitly mentions `CLI workflows`, but the corpus does not expose the install package, executable name, or exact login command.
